title: NeuralMind Community Benchmark Entry
description: One row in docs/community-benchmarks.json. Users submit a PR adding an entry to the `entries` array; CI validates against this schema.

project_name.description: Short, human-readable project name. Keep under 40 chars.

repo_url.description: Public repo URL if the project is open source. Optional — private repos are fine; you just can't link to them.

nodes.description: Total node count reported by `neuralmind stats .`. Integer, > 0.

avg_wakeup_tokens.description: Average tokens returned by `neuralmind wakeup .`. Typical range: 200–800.

avg_query_tokens.description: Average tokens returned by `neuralmind query` across a representative query set.

avg_reduction_ratio.description: Average reduction ratio vs naive 'load everything' baseline. Must be >= 1 (ratio, not percentage).

queries_per_day.description: Typical query volume, used for cost-savings estimation. Optional.

model.description: Which LLM you run NeuralMind against most often. Used for honest cost-savings calculation.

submitted_by.description: GitHub username of the submitter (no leading @).

verification_command.description: The exact command you ran to produce these numbers. Reviewers can re-run it locally to verify — this is what makes the table auditable.

notes.description: Optional additional context — e.g. 'private monorepo', 'runs on Claude Code with hooks installed', 'includes auto-generated code excluded'. Keep short.
